title: Doop/Soufflé Static Analysis Pipeline
summary: A Datalog-based points-to analysis framework for Java programs, implementing five modules: the Doop framework for declarative pointer analysis specification, the Soufflé engine for compiled Datalog execution, the porting of Doop to Soufflé for parallel high-performance analysis, a generalized k-CFA engine for deeper context sensitivity, and a field-sensitive analysis for precise heap field tracking.
instructionPreview: Implement a Datalog-based points-to analysis framework using the Soufflé Datalog engine. The workspace contains pre-extracted Java bytecode facts and type declarations.
instruction:
Implement a Datalog-based points-to analysis framework using the Soufflé Datalog engine.
The workspace contains pre-extracted Java bytecode facts and type declarations.

You will implement five modules in order:
1. Context-insensitive points-to analysis (analysis_ci.dl) with VarPointsTo, CallGraphEdge rules
2. Soufflé-optimized analysis (optimized_ci.dl) with .plan directives and folding
3. 1-call-site-sensitive analysis (analysis_cs.dl) using Soufflé record types for contexts
4. k-CFA analysis engine (analysis/kcfa.py) generalizing to k-call-site sensitivity with
   configurable depth, fixed-point iteration, context-sensitive virtual dispatch, and
   context merging for scalability
5. Field-sensitive analysis (analysis/field_sensitive.py) with precise per-field heap
   tracking, instance/static field relations, array abstraction, and type-based refinement

Modules 1-3 are Soufflé Datalog programs. Modules 4-5 are Python implementations that
model the same analysis concepts programmatically with explicit fixed-point loops.

Each module builds on previous ones. Create a run.sh that runs all implemented analyses
and reports results as KEY: VALUE lines.

Key relations: VarPointsTo(heap,var), CallGraphEdge(invocation,method),
Reachable(method), InstanceFieldPointsTo(heap,field,baseheap)

moduleDag.description: A Datalog-based points-to analysis framework for Java programs, implementing five modules: the Doop framework for declarative pointer analysis specification, the Soufflé engine for compiled Datalog execution, the porting of Doop to Soufflé for parallel high-performance analysis, a generalized k-CFA engine for deeper context sensitivity, and a field-sensitive analysis for precise heap field tracking.

nodes.label: Context-Insensitive Points-to Analysis
nodes.description: Implements Andersen-style context-insensitive points-to analysis in Soufflé Datalog. Core rules compute VarPointsTo via heap allocation, assignment, field access, virtual method dispatch, and parameter passing.

nodes.label: Soufflé-Optimized Analysis
nodes.description: Optimized version of the CI analysis using Soufflé-specific features: .plan directives for join ordering, variable reordering for efficient semi-naive evaluation, and folding transformations with intermediate relations.

nodes.label: 1-Call-Site-Sensitive Analysis
nodes.description: Context-sensitive points-to analysis using Soufflé record types for context representation. Implements 1-call-site sensitivity where each method is analyzed per call site, yielding more precise results than CI analysis.

nodes.label: k-CFA: Generalized k-Call-Site-Sensitive Analysis
nodes.description: Python-based k-CFA analysis engine implementing configurable call-string sensitivity. Maintains contexts of depth k, performs fixed-point iteration with context-sensitive virtual dispatch, parameter passing, and return value propagation. Includes context merging for scalability.

nodes.label: Field-Sensitive Points-To Analysis
nodes.description: Python-based field-sensitive analysis engine that precisely tracks which heap objects each field of each abstract object points to. Supports instance fields, static fields, and array element abstraction with type-based refinement to filter spurious entries.

edges.label: CI analysis provides base rules extended by CS analysis
Soufflé optimizations (.plan, types) used in CS analysis
1-CS analysis concepts generalized to k-CFA with configurable depth
CS analysis extended with precise per-field heap tracking
